metadata.title: Free Image Cropping, Resizing and Conversion
metadata.description: Resize, crop and convert your image for free. Use ready-made formats for social media and download JPG, PNG or WebP.

hero.title: Resize, crop and convert your image online – free editor
hero.description: Resize, crop, and convert images for Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn. Ready-made formats, circular avatars, export to JPG, PNG, and WebP. Local processing in the browser.

software.name: Image Editor – Cropping, Resizing and Conversion
software.description: Free online image editor for resizing, cropping and format conversion. Ready-made presets for Instagram, Facebook and LinkedIn. Circular avatar creation. Export to JPG, PNG and WebP with quality control. Local processing in the browser.
featureList: Ready-made Instagram formats (square post, portrait post, story, reels)
Ready-made web formats (OG image, hero, banner, thumbnail)
Crop shapes: rectangle, square, circle (round avatar)
3x3 grid (rule of thirds) with color selection
Precise crop position (X, Y) with centering

howTo.name: How to resize and crop an image for social media
howTo.description: How to resize an image for Instagram (1080x1080, 1080x1350, story), Facebook, LinkedIn, and OG image. JPG to WebP conversion and circular avatar creation.

steps.name: Add an image
steps.text: Drag a file onto the designated area or select an image from your device. Supported formats: JPG, PNG, WebP.

steps.name: Choose dimensions or a preset
steps.text: Enter target dimensions in pixels or select a ready-made format (e.g., Instagram post, Facebook cover, OG image).

steps.name: Adjust the crop
steps.text: Move and zoom the image to select the best area. The bright area in the preview shows exactly what will be saved.

steps.name: Choose format and download
steps.text: Select the file format (JPG, PNG, or WebP), set compression quality, and download the finished image.

contentBlocks.title: Resize images instantly — crop, convert and compress in one tool
contentBlocks.html:
The image editor lets you quickly adapt images to specific dimensions. You can resize an image to any pixel dimensions, select a ready-made format for social media, or crop a section of the image with precise framing.

In addition to resizing, the tool offers format conversion (JPG, PNG, WebP), circular avatar creation, and compression quality control.

contentBlocks.title: How to resize an image in seconds
contentBlocks.description: Editing an image takes just a few seconds:

items.title: 1. Add an image
items.description: Drag a file onto the designated area or select an image from your device. Supported formats: JPG, PNG, WebP.

items.title: 2. Set dimensions
items.description: Enter dimensions in pixels or select a ready-made format (Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, LinkedIn, OG image).

items.title: 3. Download
items.description: Adjust the crop, choose the file format (JPG, PNG, WebP), set quality, and download the finished image.

contentBlocks.title: How to add an image
contentBlocks.html:
The tool accepts images in JPG, PNG, and WebP formats. You can add an image in two ways:

  • Drag and drop - grab a file from a folder on your computer and drop it onto the file upload area (the area with a dashed border).
  • Select from device - clicking the file upload area opens a file selection dialog.

After adding an image, the tool automatically reads its original dimensions and displays a preview. You can now proceed to crop settings.

contentBlocks.title: Set target dimensions
contentBlocks.html: In the Dimensions in px tab, you enter width and height manually. The Keep proportions option automatically adjusts the second dimension.

contentBlocks.title: Image dimensions for Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn
contentBlocks.html:
The tool includes ready-made formats with optimal dimensions for the most popular platforms:

PlatformFormatDimensions (px)Ratio
InstagramSquare post1080 x 10801:1
InstagramPortrait post1080 x 13504:5
InstagramStory / Reels1080 x 19209:16
FacebookPost1200 x 630~1.9:1
FacebookPage cover820 x 360~2.3:1
LinkedInPost1200 x 12001:1
LinkedInProfile banner1584 x 3964:1
OG imageLink sharing1200 x 630~1.9:1

Dimensions in the table are recommended sizes for each platform. The tool automatically sets these dimensions when you select the appropriate format.

contentBlocks.title: Ready-made formats - which to choose?
contentBlocks.description: Each format corresponds to specific platform or use case requirements:

items.title: Instagram - square post (1080×1080)
items.description: Standard Instagram post format. 1:1 ratio. Works well in the feed and looks good on both phone and desktop.

items.title: Instagram - portrait post (1080×1350)
items.description: Vertical post format (4:5 ratio). Takes up more space in the feed than a square, which may increase engagement.

items.title: Instagram - story / reels (1080×1920)
items.description: Full-screen vertical format (9:16 ratio). Used in stories and reels. Fills the entire phone screen.

items.title: Facebook - post (1200×630)
items.description: Optimal format for Facebook posts and shared links. Ratio close to 16:9.

items.title: Facebook - page cover (820×360)
items.description: Background photo for a Facebook business page. Wide, panoramic format - may be cropped differently on various devices.

items.title: LinkedIn - post (1200×1200)
items.description: Square format for LinkedIn posts. Looks good in the feed and on mobile devices.

items.title: LinkedIn - profile banner (1584×396)
items.description: Background image for a personal or company LinkedIn profile. Very wide format - center the key element in the crop.

items.title: OG image (1200×630)
items.description: Graphic displayed when sharing a link on social media (Open Graph). Standard for Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, and other platforms.

items.title: Article graphic (1600×900)
items.description: Wide format suitable for blog article headers. 16:9 ratio looks good on text-heavy pages.

items.title: Website banner (1920×600)
items.description: Very wide format for website banners. Works as a background under headers or in offer sections.

items.title: Article thumbnail (800×600)
items.description: Smaller format for thumbnails on article lists, product carousels, or search result previews.

items.title: Hero section (1920×1080)
items.description: Full HD full-screen format. Used as the main section background on homepages or offer pages.

contentBlocks.title: How to crop an image
contentBlocks.html:
After setting target dimensions, an interactive crop area appears on the preview. The bright part of the image is the section that will be saved - the rest is dimmed.

Dragging the crop

Grab the bright area and drag it to any part of the image. This way you choose which section of the photo will be exported.

Resizing via handles

In the corners of the crop area there are small squares (handles). Dragging them changes the crop size - you can enlarge or shrink it while maintaining the chosen proportions.

Zoom

In the Zoom tab you will find a slider to adjust zoom (100–300%). A higher value means the crop covers a smaller section of the original image - useful when you want to cut out a specific detail.

Precise position control

In the Position tab you can set the exact crop position in percentages (0–100% for X and Y axes). Centering buttons let you quickly position the crop at the center of the image.

contentBlocks.title: Crop shapes
contentBlocks.demoHtml: RectangleSquareCircle
contentBlocks.html:
In the Crop Shapes tab, you choose the shape of the exported image: rectangle (with selected proportions), square (forces 1:1), or circle (with transparent background).

The circle shape creates a round avatar with a transparent background outside the circle. The tool automatically switches the format to PNG or WebP, since JPG does not support transparency.

contentBlocks.title: 3×3 grid - what is it for?
contentBlocks.html:
A grid dividing the image into 9 equal parts is visible on the crop area. This is a visualization of the rule of thirds - one of the fundamental principles of photographic composition.

The rule states that the most important elements of a photo (face, product, point of interest) should be placed at the intersections of the grid lines or along them. Such composition is more dynamic and pleasing to the eye than placing the subject exactly in the center.

In the Grid color tab you can change the line color (green, white, black, red, yellow) so the grid is clearly visible on different images.

tabs.title: JPG
tabs.html:
The most popular format for photos. Good compression while maintaining visual quality. Does not support transparency - the background will always be filled with a color.

A good choice for product photos, portraits, and most website graphics. The quality slider (60–100%) controls compression.

tabs.title: PNG
tabs.html:
Lossless format - preserves full quality, but files are larger. Supports transparency (required for the circle shape).

A good choice for graphics with text, icons, and images requiring sharp edges.

tabs.title: WebP
tabs.html:
Modern format combining the advantages of JPG and PNG - small files, good quality, transparency support. Supported by all modern browsers.

Recommended for websites - smaller files mean faster loading.

contentBlocks.title: Aspect ratios - what do they mean?
contentBlocks.description: The aspect ratio is the relationship between the width and height of an image. Written as two numbers separated by a colon:

items.title: 1:1 (square)
items.description: Width equals height. Instagram square post, profile pictures, icons.

items.title: 4:5 (portrait)
items.description: Slightly vertical format. Instagram portrait post - takes up more space in the feed than a square.

items.title: 3:2 (classic)
items.description: Traditional analog photography proportions. Many digital cameras also use this format.

items.title: 16:9 (widescreen)
items.description: Standard HD video, presentation, and monitor format. YouTube, Facebook video, website banners.

items.title: 9:16 (vertical full-screen)
items.description: Inverted 16:9 - a vertical format filling the entire phone screen. Instagram Stories, TikTok, YouTube Shorts.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about the image editor

items.question: Does the editor handle very large images?
items.answer: Yes, though processing may be slower for images above 4000×4000 pixels - this depends on the device's power and available browser memory. All processing happens locally, without sending files to a server.
items.answerSchemaText: Yes. Processing may be slower above 4000×4000 px. All processing is local.

items.question: Which export format to choose - JPG, PNG, or WebP?
items.answer: JPG is a good choice for photos with many colors and gradients - files are small with good quality. PNG preserves the highest quality and supports transparency (e.g., circular avatar). WebP combines the advantages of both - small files with high quality and transparency support.
items.answerSchemaText: JPG for photos, PNG for transparency, WebP combines small files with high quality.

items.question: How to create a circular profile picture?
items.answer: In the Crop Shapes tab, select the Circle option. The tool will automatically switch the format to PNG or WebP (JPG does not support transparency). The area outside the circle will be transparent.
items.answerSchemaText: Select Circle in the Crop Shapes tab. The area outside the circle is transparent.

items.question: What are the image dimensions for Instagram?
items.answer: Instagram supports three main formats: square post (1080×1080 px, 1:1 ratio), portrait post (1080×1350 px, 4:5 ratio), and story/reels (1080×1920 px, 9:16 ratio). The tool has ready-made formats for each of these dimensions.
items.answerSchemaText: Square post: 1080×1080 px, portrait post: 1080×1350 px, story/reels: 1080×1920 px.

items.question: How does the quality slider work?
items.answer: The quality slider (60–100%) controls the compression level for JPG and WebP formats. A higher value means better image quality but a larger file size. For most social media use cases, the optimal value is 70–85%.
items.answerSchemaText: The slider controls JPG and WebP compression. 70–85% is optimal for social media.

items.question: Why is JPG format unavailable with the circle shape?
items.answer: JPG format does not support transparency. The circle shape requires a transparent background outside the circle, so the tool automatically limits the choice to PNG or WebP - formats with an alpha channel.
items.answerSchemaText: JPG does not support transparency. Circle shape requires PNG or WebP.

items.question: What do aspect ratios like 4:5 or 16:9 mean?
items.answer: Aspect ratios define the relationship between the width and height of an image. For example, 4:5 means the width is 4 parts and the height is 5 parts. 16:9 is the typical ratio for films and presentations. Instagram portrait post uses 4:5, while story and reels use 9:16.
items.answerSchemaText: 4:5 = width 4 parts, height 5 parts. 16:9 = standard widescreen.
